operations.rationale: Replace the proof-receipt lifecycle table with reader prose while preserving the canonical AI/research receipt-state matrix.
content_lines: A proof receipt moves through transport states, not capability states. A theorem-linked receipt names a theorem reference and proof-status scope, but it supports only structural discussion until resolver status is known. A dictionary-bound receipt ties the finite model to a recognized contract family, which makes it useful for receipt design and local surrogate tests.

A fingerprinted receipt has recorded content or normalized parameters, so it may become a replay candidate, but it is still not quality evidence. A resolver-checked receipt has passed or failed a stated theorem-resolution command. A consumer-gated receipt records allowed and blocked downstream uses, and those uses remain scoped to the named consumer.

Workload-blocked is the most important reader-facing state: it means the structural receipt may exist, but workload, baseline, metric, or evidence artifacts are missing, so no quality, runtime, memory, or transfer promotion is allowed. Retired or superseded receipts become historical evidence only unless a later review requalifies them.
These states keep proof authority and consumer authority separate. A receipt can carry a narrow structural fact without carrying a model-quality claim.

operations.rationale: Replace the dense Circle receipt-boundary table with reader prose while preserving exact public-safe receipt facts, fingerprints, theorem IDs, validator boundaries, and non-claims.
content_lines: The public-safe Circle lane is concrete enough to show as a receipt boundary, but not as a capability result. The imported record points to an external Circle checkout at commit `63b0f511`, contract `CC-AI-CONTRACT-ROPE-001`, family `rope_position_distinguishability`, and requested margin `1/328459`. The certifier record says `status proved`, `request_passed true`, `decision passed`, `assurance mixed_theorem_and_computation`, and `theorem_count 55`; the ready digest reports `ready=True fields=31 missing=0 theorems=75`.

The deterministic fields remain narrow: `evidence.d19_proved_request_status="proved"`, `evidence.d19_proved_first_channel_bank_transfer=true`, `evidence.real_phase_dirichlet_witness_guardrail=true`, `evidence.exact_discrete_pass=true`, and `evidence.total_bank_collision_pair_count=0`. The ASI consumer gate checks seven theorem IDs for this receipt boundary: `AIRA-T0058`, `AIRA-T0059`, `AIRA-T0171`, `AIRA-T0172`, `AIRA-T0239`, `AIRA-T0240`, and `AIRA-T0241`.

The receipt also carries replay-facing fingerprints: receipt `91b72a6dcf821a9733f21800cd1093a3d0665588022031ba72c94893800330c3`, normalized request `20e68c5f787e267c6611bc57b8d8e98e1cb0f5a74f272379716a5d83e761407d`, contract pack `df673f8a661fc89a26372685986c92f2221aaa617d6738fce5c2a76bd5d0eeae`, and contract `a0f35d3e89e9b6eac555f0392450f4f75cf7e70f30cff44ec7434f61bd85b468`. The recorded external test batch says `145 passed in 718.24s (0:11:58)`, and the ASI consumer gate rejects digest mismatch, missing required theorem ID, stale contract fingerprint status, and unsupported transfer-claim use.

Those specifics matter because they keep the receipt inspectable. They also keep it bounded. This lane does not prove model quality, reasoning ability, context length, speed, memory scaling, deployment safety, transfer, or ASI. It does not prove deployed proof-contract transport inside The ASI Stack, and it does not promote any chapter core claim above `argument`.

section.title: Beyond the State of the Art

operations.rationale: Replace the dense proof-contract transport target architecture with reader prose while preserving the canonical AI/research receipt-field requirements.
content_lines: In its finished form, Circle proof-carrying contracts would act as a transport service for small structural facts. A finite fact could move through the ASI Stack with its theorem reference, deterministic fields, fingerprint, resolver state, replay expectation, consumer gate, and non-claims still attached.

The point is not to make proof language grander. It is to make it harder for proof language to drift. A receipt should let another layer use a structural fact while also seeing exactly what the fact does not establish: model quality, real-valued deployment behavior, runtime efficiency, memory improvement, downstream usefulness, or broad safety.

The mature transport lane would keep receipt states separate from capability states. A receipt may be theorem-linked, fingerprinted, resolver-checked, consumer-gated, workload-blocked, retired, or superseded; none of those states is a measured model result. Stale theorem IDs become residuals, fingerprint mismatches block replay, and workload-blocked receipts preserve structure without promotion.

The book has not demonstrated that transport service. The support state remains `argument` until receipt records, theorem-resolution checks, replay status, consumer-gate tests, stale-proof failure behavior, and downstream promotion reviews show that proof-carrying claims can travel without overstating what the proof stack established.
